fundamentals.name: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc. provides cybersecurity solutions in the United States and internationally. Its unified platform provides cloud-delivered protection of endpoints, cloud workloads, identity, and data through a software as a service (SaaS) subscription-based model. The company offers corporate endpoint and cloud workload security, managed security, security and vulnerability management, IT operations management, identity protection, threat intelligence, data protection, SaaS security posture management, and AI powered workflow automation, and securing generative AI workload services, as well as security orchestration, automation, and response; and security information and event management, and log management services. It primarily sells subscriptions to its Falcon platform and cloud modules. The company was incorporated in 2011 and is headquartered in Austin, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing cybersecurity company with strong free cash flow but a high valuation and negative profit margin.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 1.6%; next earnings date is 2026-06-03.
positives: Revenue growth 23.3% YoY, with analyst estimates for current Q and FY showing strong expected growth.
Gross margin 74.8% indicates strong pricing power and efficient service delivery.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $1.60B demonstrates operational efficiency and cash generation.
Total cash of $5.23B provides ample liquidity, with total debt at $820.08M.
negatives: Forward P/E of 107.73 and Price/Sales of 35.09 indicate a very high valuation.
Profit margin is negative at -3.4%, and Return on Equity and Assets are also negative.
Mean analyst target of $527.63 is significantly below the current spot price of $663.46.
Debt/Equity ratio of 18.34% is relatively high, though manageable given cash reserves.

news.paragraph: CrowdStrike is seeing significant positive attention, with the stock earning a spot on an elite list and cyber stocks, including CRWD, Cisco, and Palo Alto, outperforming semiconductor stocks to hit records. Morgan Stanley has also significantly revised its price target for CrowdStrike, while the company announced visibility and monitoring for Claude activity via Claude's Compliance API. The stock is trading at all-time highs ahead of its next earnings report on June 3rd.
